title: Market Brief — Thursday, April 16, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across the board. H100s sit at 18% availability (70 of 303 GPUs) and H200s at a severe 13% (49 of 325), while H100 spot jumped 7.1% day-over-day to $2.00/hr as H200s slid 7.1% to $3.36. B200 volatility at 96.1% annualized signals a generation-transition market still hunting for equilibrium, and utilization is dropping fast — H100 down 9.5pp, B200 down 16.7pp in 30 days — suggesting demand destruction at these prices, not loosening supply.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across the board. H100s sit at 18% availability (70 of 303 GPUs) and H200s at a severe 13% (49 of 325), while H100 spot jumped 7.1% day-over-day to $2.00/hr as H200s slid 7.1% to $3.36. B200 volatility at 96.1% annualized signals a generation-transition market still hunting for equilibrium, and utilization is dropping fast — H100 down 9.5pp, B200 down 16.7pp in 30 days — suggesting demand destruction at these prices, not loosening supply.

Inference margins are brutal at the Llama 70B reference cost. TCO-adjusted H100 cost is $7.31/M tokens and H200 is $8.04/M, meaning mid-tier API providers selling at $6/M are burning cash. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M are selling far below self-hosted TCO — DeepSeek is losing roughly $6.93 per million tokens against H100 economics. Provider grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

H200 spot is undervalued at $3.36/hr given 13% availability and tightening supply — buy H200 capacity before the squeeze reprices it toward OCPI fair value of $3.04 plus scarcity premium. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued when GPT-5.4 benchmarks competitively at $15/M — fade premium reasoning tier exposure.
bullets: 18% of H100 GPUs are available, classifying compute as scarce at 70 free units out of 303.
13% H200 availability marks a severe shortage with only 49 of 325 GPUs free.
$2.00/hr H100 spot median represents a 7.1% single-day price spike.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 is the cheapest inference available across all 18 tracked models.
96.1% annualized volatility on B200 OCPI makes next-gen GPU pricing the most unstable asset in the stack.
